name: Triangulate
tagline: Separation of duties for AI work — no consensus, no sign-off
plain_summary: Triangulate runs independent reviewer roles plus a deterministic auditor over the same AI-assisted workpaper. When they disagree, the line stops and the work goes to a person. It is separation of duties applied to AI: an explicit authority hierarchy, not a multi-model majority vote.
marketing_thesis: A processor buyer cannot re-run the vendor's benchmark lab. Every figure on this page re-derives from the public repository — the benchmark is a command, not a press release.

layers.label: Human Gate
layers.plain: The demo marks work eligible or blocked; a person makes the real-world approval decision.
layers.engineering: HumanGate is a deterministic policy class: Critical → FAIL, High → FLAG, and Medium/Low → PASS with residual notes. PASS is eligibility for human sign-off, not a recorded human signature.

layers.label: Deterministic Auditor
layers.plain: Re-does the arithmetic itself, so no model is asked whether a number 'looks right.'
layers.engineering: Mechanically re-derives every formula on a hashed clone; a tie-out mismatch is a Critical finding regardless of model opinion.

layers.label: Reviewer
layers.plain: Challenges the draft and flags issues — read-only, it cannot change the file.
layers.engineering: Read-only reviewer role; the orchestrator hashes the workpaper before and after so a reviewer cannot mutate what it inspects.

layers.label: Specialist
layers.plain: A narrow second opinion or source analysis where the work needs one.
layers.engineering: Specialist role supplies scoped second opinions; ranks below source data and signed work in the authority model.

layers.label: Preparer
layers.plain: Builds the first draft of the workpaper.
layers.engineering: Preparer role produces the draft that the other roles then challenge; no self-review.

blocks.label: Preparer
blocks.engineering: DemoPreparer builds draft; offline deterministic in the public demo

blocks.label: Reviewer
blocks.plain: challenges the draft without changing it

blocks.label: Specialist
blocks.plain: adds a narrow second opinion where needed

blocks.label: Reconcile + Policy
blocks.plain: combines findings and applies the severity policy
blocks.engineering: findings reconciled; deterministic severity policy returns PASS, FLAG, or FAIL

blocks.label: Human Approval
blocks.plain: a person signs off — or doesn't
blocks.engineering: external approval boundary; the demo policy does not record or replace a human signature

spec_strip.label: Tests
spec_strip.source: python -m pytest --collect-only -q (ai-validation-framework/); verified 2026-07-20 and pinned in site-datasheets/counts.json
spec_strip.plain: the checks that pin this engine's behavior

spec_strip.label: Roles
spec_strip.source: ai-validation-framework/README.md — Preparer, Reviewer, Specialist, Deterministic audit, Policy gate + human approver

spec_strip.label: Gate exit codes
spec_strip.source: ai-validation-framework/README.md — "exits 0 on PASS and non-zero on FLAG/FAIL"
spec_strip.plain: so it can act as a CI gate

spec_strip.label: Default mode
spec_strip.source: ai-validation-framework/README.md — "default demo needs no API key and no network access"

spec_strip.label: Determinism
spec_strip.source: ai-validation-framework/README.md — "All sample data is fictional and seeded"; read-only reviewer

problem_statement: No high-stakes workpaper should rely on one AI system reporting "looks good." Models agree too easily, invent support, miss the same issue twice, and explain a number without tying it to source. Triangulate assumes those failures will happen and builds the workflow around them.

scenarios.title: An injected hallucination is caught by the arithmetic, not an opinion
scenarios.narrative: In the adversarial demo, an AI asserts a Total Revenue $49,000 over what the revenue streams actually sum to, backed only by an 'AI assumption' — the lowest authority. Because that cell feeds tax and net, the single bad figure cascades. The Reviewer and the deterministic Auditor both flag it independently; the verdict is FAIL and the process exits non-zero.

scenarios.title: The gate discriminates — a clean workpaper passes
scenarios.narrative: The clean sample runs the identical flow and returns PASS, proving the gate discriminates instead of always failing.

scenarios.title: Arithmetic self-heals; judgment escalates
scenarios.narrative: The review loop re-derives the lowest broken formula cell from its own formula on a clone, then re-runs the full pipeline. The injected $49k error clears in one turn (FAIL → PASS). An AI-assumption input and a hardcoded cell have nothing to re-derive from, so they stay in the fix packet for a human (FAIL → FLAG).

instruction_set.artifacts: builder memo, fix packet, change log, QA summary, verdict.json

instruction_set.operation: inject one hallucinated figure and catch it
write the audit trail to a chosen directory
print the review without writing files
attempt repair and escalate unsupported judgment
choose the loop's deterministic data seed

benchmarks.label: Adversarial cascade depth
benchmarks.plain: one bad number, six downstream failures caught

benchmarks.label: Engine tests
benchmarks.source: python -m pytest --collect-only -q (ai-validation-framework/); verified 2026-07-20 and pinned in site-datasheets/counts.json

authority.note: highest authority in the implemented enum
documented decision from the engagement record
existing formula logic, subject to re-derivation
lowest authority; cannot outrank cited evidence

verdict_map.action: fix packet escalated to a human
residual notes documented; eligible for human sign-off; exit 0

guarantees: Read-only reviewer: the orchestrator hashes the workpaper before and after review steps, so a reviewer cannot mutate what it inspects.
Deterministic audit: every formula is mechanically re-derived; arithmetic decides, not model opinion.
Seeded, fictional data throughout the public demo.

gate_policy.note: The demo's HumanGate class applies the severity policy; an actual person remains the final approver.

modes.name: Enterprise-safe mode (default)
modes.detail: deterministic mock reviewer, no API key, no network call, runnable in conservative corporate IT.

modes.name: Agent-accelerated mode
modes.detail: where approved, a live Anthropic Claude reviewer adapter can replace the mock reviewer; an optional orchestration layer for approved, agent-enabled environments; the platform runs fully without it.

limits.statement: The loop clears arithmetic; it cannot manufacture authority. An AI-assumption input or a hardcoded cell with no formula has nothing to re-derive from — those stay in the fix packet for a human.
The framework does not apply changes on its own or override its own verdict; final sign-off authority is held by a person.
The public demo runs offline with a deterministic mock reviewer; live-model results depend on the pinned model approved for that client environment.

crops.label: Verdict and severity summary
crops.alt: Triangulate terminal excerpt showing FAIL, six Critical findings, one Medium finding, and the automated-policy demo gate

crops.label: Fix packet with independent detections
crops.alt: Triangulate terminal excerpt showing Reviewer and Deterministic Auditor tie-out findings for the injected fictional error

meta.description: Triangulate (SFS-E06-TRI) datasheet — separation of duties for AI-assisted financial workpapers: independent reviewer roles plus a deterministic auditor, an automated policy verdict at an explicit human-approval boundary, and every figure re-derivable from the public repository.
